factors.name: SpaceX IPO (mega-cap tech event)
factors.summary: SpaceX completed record $75B IPO on Nasdaq Friday with shares up 19% (+25% intraday), becoming ~$2.2T market cap company. This is a major positive validation for Nasdaq as venue and could attract institutional flows into tech mega-caps; however, the move is complete and SpaceX proxy stocks are now unwinding (late-stage dynamic).

factors.name: Iran peace deal framework (risk-on macro backdrop)
factors.summary: US-Iran negotiations reportedly near framework deal with Hormuz reopening and nuclear inspections included; at least $10B in frozen Iranian funds unlocked. Risk-off pressures ease, supporting equities and reducing safe-haven demand, though deal still unsigned heading into weekend (uncertainty remains).

factors.name: Paramount-WBD merger DOJ approval
factors.summary: Justice Department cleared ~$110B Paramount-Warner Bros merger, removing antitrust overhang. Positive for media/streaming but minimal direct impact on Nasdaq 100 composition (no mega-cap tech names directly affected).

factors.name: US government AI export controls on Anthropic
factors.summary: US ordered Anthropic to halt foreign access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models citing national security; China also labeled additional tech firms as 'military' by US. These actions create regulatory headwind for AI sector and Nasdaq-listed AI-adjacent names; ongoing China-US tech tensions.

factors.name: Roku strategic review (sale possibility)
factors.summary: Roku exploring strategic options including potential sale; typical M&A noise with unclear timeline and impact on broader Nasdaq. Mid-cap situation, not a major NDX driver.

factors.name: Price action (4h / 24h momentum)
factors.summary: NAS100 +0.46% over 24h, -0.22% over 4h showing choppy consolidation. No decisive directional momentum; price action is flat-to-mixed into weekend with SpaceX euphoria already priced in Friday.

factors.name: Fed policy and rate expectations (forward-looking)
factors.summary: No major Fed data or FOMC decision in immediate window. BoJ rate hike (25 bps) expected Tuesday; BoE and RBA meetings also pending next week. Lack of imminent US inflation or Fed data leaves rate repricing risk muted near-term.

factors.name: 10-year Treasury yield and real yields
factors.summary: No specific Treasury yield data in news window; gold pushed above $4,200 on Iran deal optimism and inflation-hedging, suggesting real yields may be under pressure. However, no concrete yield move disclosed to confirm directional bias for duration-sensitive Nasdaq mega-caps.

factors.name: Inflation expectations and CPI calendar
factors.summary: No recent CPI, PCE, or Core CPI releases in the news window. Social Security COLA for 2027 may reach 4.7% if inflation persists, but this is forward-looking and not an immediate catalyst. Retail sales and FOMC data incoming next week.

factors.name: Semiconductor supply chain and chip export controls
factors.summary: China-US tech tensions escalating: US labeled more Chinese firms as 'military'; China 'strongly dissatisfied' with Pentagon move. Export controls on AI chips and restricted access to advanced models (Anthropic) could pressure NVDA, TSMC, semiconductor ETFs; geopolitical overhang.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 closed Friday mixed-to-positive on SpaceX IPO euphoria (record $75B raise, 19% opening-day gain) and Iran peace deal optimism, but momentum is already fading into the weekend. Positive sentiment from reduced geopolitical risk (Hormuz reopening prospect) and record venture success on the Nasdaq is offset by escalating US-China tech tensions (AI export controls on Anthropic, military designations, chip supply chain friction). The market is waiting for next week's central bank calendar (BoJ Tuesday, BoE/RBA/FOMC later) and retail sales/inflation data to reset directional bias; without fresh catalysts, price action is consolidative and choppy.
catalysts_ahead: BoJ rate decision + commentary (Tuesday 2026-06-17)
BoE and RBA meetings (later next week)
US retail sales data (next week)
FOMC meeting and dot plot (next week)
Nvidia, Microsoft, Apple, Tesla earnings (if scheduled imminent)
Iran deal signing confirmation (possibly this weekend or early next week)
China policy/tariff response to US tech restrictions
screening.risk_warning: If Iran deal collapses or talks break down, geopolitical risk-off could flush equities fast. Alternatively, if Fed signals looser policy stance at FOMC, 10-year yields compress sharply lower, supporting long-duration Nasdaq mega-caps and invalidating near-term neutral bias. US-China chip wars escalation (more export bans, stricter DMA enforcement in EU) could trigger sector rotation away from mega-cap tech; monitor antitrust news and NVDA supply chain commentary closely.
screening.entry_guidance: Market is in consolidation after SpaceX and Iran euphoria; avoid chasing into weekend. Wait for Monday open and reaction to BoJ Tuesday for fresh directional signal; if NAS100 breaks above 29,800 with conviction and BoJ dovish, consider long; if breaks below 29,500 on renewed China-US tech tension, short setup emerges.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO Mega-Event
factors.summary: SpaceX completed largest IPO on record ($75B raise, $2.2T market cap) with shares up 19% on Friday debut. Historic milestone likely to drive broad sentiment lift and index inclusion flows into Nasdaq-100.

factors.name: Iran-US Peace Deal Progress
factors.summary: Reports indicate framework agreement near signing to reopen Strait of Hormuz, reducing geopolitical premium. Markets rallied Friday on deal optimism; crude fell below $85, reducing inflation tail-risk.

factors.name: Anthropic AI Model Shutdown (US Order)
factors.summary: US government directive forced Anthropic to disable Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models on national security grounds. Signals tightening AI export controls and regulatory headwinds for US AI champions—potential multiple compression risk.

factors.name: China-US Tech Tensions Escalating
factors.summary: Pentagon designated major Chinese tech firms as military-linked; China rebuked US. Ongoing export controls and geopolitical friction remain a structural overhang on semiconductor and cloud capex outlooks.

factors.name: Fed/Rate Expectations & Macro Calendar
factors.summary: No major macro data or FOMC decision in the immediate window (24-72h). Central bank decisions (BoJ, BoE, RBA, Fed) loom next week but are not imminent. Real yields and nominal yields stable in context.

factors.name: Mega-Cap Earnings & Guidance
factors.summary: No major Nasdaq-100 earnings releases or hyperscaler capex announcements in news window. SpaceX IPO success does not directly move AAPL/MSFT/GOOGL/NVDA fundamentals but may lift risk-on sentiment broadly.

factors.name: Crypto Stabilization & BTC Recovery
factors.summary: Bitcoin recovered from $59K lows to trade above $63K-$64K; Standard Chartered calls crypto bottom in. Bitcoin strength historically correlates with Nasdaq-100 risk-on flows; positive backdrop for growth equities.

factors.name: Short-Term Price Action (4h/24h)
factors.summary: NAS100 +0.46% (24h), -0.22% (4h) shows muted momentum; no dramatic directional conviction yet. SpaceX IPO euphoria occurred Friday but Nasdaq-100 index itself did not surge dramatically on close.

factors.name: DOJ Paramount-WBD Merger Approval
factors.summary: DOJ green-lit $110B deal Friday, removing near-term antitrust overhang. Signals regulatory environment less hostile to M&A; may ease antitrust concerns around large-cap tech consolidation.

factors.name: Value vs. Growth Rotation Narrative
factors.summary: MarketWatch report notes value stocks outperforming growth by wide margin YTD. Potential headwind if growth multiple compression continues; Nasdaq-100 (heavy growth/tech) more vulnerable than Russell 1000 Value.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq-100 is treading water in a mixed macro backdrop dominated by two conflicting narratives: (1) SpaceX IPO record success and potential Iran peace deal reducing geopolitical tail-risk, both BULLISH for risk sentiment and growth valuations; (2) Anthropic AI model shutdowns and escalating China-US tech/chip export controls, both BEARISH for long-duration AI/semiconductor plays that comprise >25% of NDX. Friday's modest 24h gain (+0.46%) and weak 4h action (-0.22%) suggest the index is consolidating rather than committed to a breakout. No major economic data or Fed signals have landed to reprice rates, leaving real yields stable but vulnerable.
catalysts_ahead: Retail Sales data (mid-week)
FOMC meeting conclusion (mid-late week)
NVDA, MSFT, AAPL, GOOGL earnings (if scheduled within 72h)
PCE/Core PCE inflation print (if released this cycle)
Iran deal formal signing (likely next week per Trump admin)
Nasdaq-100 inclusion of SpaceX proxy trading flows (ongoing)
screening.risk_warning: BULLISH bias invalidated if: (1) Iran deal collapses or signing delayed, reigniting geopolitical premium and crude spike; (2) CPI/PCE surprise materially hot, forcing Fed re-pricing and real yield spike; (3) mega-cap earnings disappoint or capex guidance pulls back; (4) China escalates tech restrictions or Taiwan tensions spike. BEARISH bias invalidated if: Fed signals dovish pivot, rate cuts accelerate, or AI regulatory concerns prove overblown.
screening.entry_guidance: No clear entry conviction yet. Wait for: either a 1%+ break above 29900 resistance on volume (bullish breakout), or a pullback to 29300 support on continued weakness (better risk/reward long entry). Given mixed headlines, recommend holding fire until either FOMC/macro data clarity or earnings catalyst emerges.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO mega-event
factors.summary: SpaceX completed $75B IPO (largest ever), shares +19% on debut, valuation ~$2.2T. Massive wealth creation event and risk-on sentiment for Nasdaq-listed mega-caps; however, euphoria often precedes consolidation. Market may be pricing in this narrative already.

factors.name: Iran peace deal framework (geopolitical risk-off)
factors.summary: US-Iran interim deal framework reportedly close, with Hormuz reopening and $10-20B in frozen Iranian funds to be unlocked. Reduced geopolitical tail risk supports equity risk appetite and long-duration growth multiples (positive for NAS100).

factors.name: AI regulation & export controls tightening
factors.summary: US government disabled Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models via export control directive; China rebuked US military labeling of top tech firms. Regulatory headwinds on AI capex and foreign access could pressure mega-cap AI revenue expectations (NVDA, MSFT, META).

factors.name: Fed policy & rate expectations (unchanged near-term)
factors.summary: No major Fed surprise in news window. ECB's Nagel comments on persistent inflation suggest higher-for-longer, but no imminent US rate shift signaled. Real yields stable; growth multiple compression risk muted for now.

factors.name: 10Y Treasury yield (no fresh data)
factors.summary: No new Treasury or inflation data in 24h window. Without CPI/PCE print or FOMC, yield trajectory unclear; tech long-duration sensitivity remains latent.

factors.name: VIX / volatility sentiment
factors.summary: SpaceX euphoria and Iran deal optimism suggest risk-on tone, but NAS100 price action is modest (+0.46% 24h, -0.22% 4h). No volatility spike or squeeze signal; market digesting catalysts calmly.

factors.name: Dollar strength (USD impact on NQ international revenue)
factors.summary: DXY ~99.80 (down 0.27% on week per FXStreet). No major USD tailwind or headwind; most NQ components have healthy international diversification but near-term FX backdrop is neutral.

factors.name: Crypto correlation (risk-on proxy)
factors.summary: Bitcoin recovered to $63-64k range after $59k low earlier in week; Standard Chartered calls crypto bottom in place. Risk-on sentiment reflected in BTC strength should support NQ risk assets.

factors.name: Tech sentiment & earnings visibility
factors.summary: No mega-cap earnings or forward guidance in news window. SpaceX IPO is SpaceX-specific event, not Nasdaq index constituent yet. NVDA, MSFT, AAPL earnings cycle still distant; no visibility surprises.

factors.name: Antitrust & regulatory approvals
factors.summary: DOJ approved Paramount-WBD merger (~$110B deal), clearing a major deal uncertainty. No new antitrust actions on mega-caps reported; regulatory backdrop mildly constructive.

screening.narrative: NAS100 is caught between two competing narratives: SpaceX's record $75B IPO and imminent Iran peace deal both support risk-on sentiment and long-duration growth multiples (bullish for tech); however, tightening AI export controls and regulatory scrutiny on mega-cap AI capex are headwinds. Price action is muted (+0.46% 24h), suggesting market is pricing in catalysts but not yet committing to a directional breakout. Fresh geopolitical stability could unlock a relief rally, but AI regulation risks cap upside.
catalysts_ahead: Retail sales data (US, likely early next week)
FOMC meeting decision (likely mid-late June per typical calendar)
CPI / PCE inflation print (next 10-14 days)
NVDA, MSFT, AAPL earnings dates (check July calendar)
Iran deal signing (expected next few days, per Trump admin comment)
China policy response to US tech export controls
screening.risk_warning: If Iran deal falls apart or geopolitical tensions re-escalate, risk-off reversal could trigger 1–2% NAS100 dip. Conversely, if AI export controls ease or mega-cap earnings beat expectations, upside to 30,200–30,500 is possible. Lack of major data release in next 24h means event risk is muted; watch for surprise inflation print or Fed guidance shift.
screening.entry_guidance: Market is in early-stage risk-on phase but price action is tepid (move not yet confirmed). On weakness to 29,400–29,500, consider long entry with target 30,000 if Iran deal signed and AI regulation fears ease. Avoid chasing near resistance; await pullback or fresh catalyst confirmation.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO mega-event
factors.summary: SpaceX completed landmark $75B IPO on Nasdaq Friday, raising $2.2T market cap (6th most valuable US company), delivering 19% first-day gain and boosting risk-on sentiment. Creates positive momentum for mega-cap tech and venture-backed growth narratives.

factors.name: Iran-US peace deal optimism
factors.summary: Reports indicate US-Iran interim deal framework likely to be signed within 24 hours per Pakistan PM, with $10-20B in frozen Iranian funds to be unlocked and Hormuz strait reopening expected. Reduces geopolitical tail-risk premium and eases inflation concerns tied to energy disruption.

factors.name: AI regulation / export controls headwind
factors.summary: US government issued directive forcing Anthropic to disable flagship AI models (Fable 5, Mythos 5) citing national security; China labeled additional firms as 'military companies' by US. Creates uncertainty around AI monetization and hyperscaler capex ROI, pressuring growth-stock valuations.

factors.name: Valuation skepticism (tech mega-caps)
factors.summary: Bloomberg article 'Can Tech Justify a Trillion-Dollar Valuation?' raises structural questions on mega-cap pricing; Pimco warns equity valuations look stretched and advocates fixed income as portfolio anchor. Suggests crowding and stretched multiples in NQ exposure.

factors.name: Paramount-WBD merger DOJ approval
factors.summary: DOJ approved ~$110B Paramount-WBD merger, removing regulatory overhang. Positive for media consolidation narrative but immaterial to core Nasdaq 100 mega-cap fundamentals.

factors.name: Fed policy / rate expectations
factors.summary: No major Fed speakers or FOMC announcements in the past 24h news window. Kevin Warsh appointed as Fed chairman but no immediate policy guidance released. Lack of fresh rate catalysts keeps nominal/real yield outlook flat near-term.

factors.name: Real yields / 10-year Treasury
factors.summary: No material DXY or Treasury yield moves reported in the 24h window. USD Index was down 0.27% for the week (~99.80 level). Gold cleared $4,200 on peace-trade optimism but no spike in real yields to re-price growth multiples.

factors.name: CPI / inflation data
factors.summary: No CPI, PCE, or Core CPI prints released in the news window. Recent commentary hints inflation may stay elevated longer if Middle East conflict eases (ECB's Nagel); Social Security COLA estimate for 2027 could reach 4.7%. No fresh inflation surprise to drive repricing.

factors.name: Semiconductor / NVDA demand
factors.summary: No earnings updates or supply-chain news for TSMC, ASML, Samsung, or NVDA in the 24h window. South Korean semiconductor strength noted (KOSPI up on AI-led rally) but lacks specificity on order trends or forward guidance.

factors.name: Crypto correlation / BTC strength
factors.summary: Bitcoin rallied to $64,349 session high on Friday as risk sentiment improved; Standard Chartered calls crypto cycle bottom in, pointing to $100K year-end target. Risk-on backdrop historically supports NQ growth names.

factors.name: Price action phase (4h -0.22%, 24h +0.46%)
factors.summary: NAS100 up only 0.46% over 24h with -0.22% in last 4h suggests consolidation, not strong directional conviction. Move too small to trigger Phase 2 extension; lacks momentum follow-through from SpaceX/Iran optimism.

screening.narrative: SpaceX's record $75B IPO and emerging US-Iran peace framework unleashed bullish sentiment for risk-on growth and de-escalation trades on Friday, but Nasdaq 100 has barely budged (+0.46% in 24h), suggesting the move is already priced or investors are waiting for confirmation on deal signing and macro clarity. Concurrent headwinds from AI regulation (Anthropic model shutdowns) and skepticism on mega-cap valuations (Pimco, Bloomberg) are offsetting bullish catalysts. The index is treading water into the weekend with no fresh Fed speakers, inflation data, or mega-cap earnings guidance to drive directional conviction.
catalysts_ahead: US-Iran deal official signing (expected within 24–72h per Pakistan PM)
Fed speakers and rate guidance (Kevin Warsh tenure; FOMC meeting likely early July)
Mega-cap earnings season (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, META, NVDA dates TBD)
PCE or CPI inflation print (next scheduled release likely mid-late June)
Hyperscaler cloud capex and AI guidance (AWS, Azure, GCP earnings calls)
AI regulation updates (US export control enforcement on Chinese firms, EU DMA actions)
screening.risk_warning: Bias is NEUTRAL with low confidence (5/10) because bullish catalysts (SpaceX IPO, Iran deal) have not yet translated into sustained NAS100 price action, and bearish risks (AI export controls, valuation concerns, stretched multiples) remain unresolved. A sudden collapse in Iran deal negotiations or hawkish Fed pivot would flip bias BEARISH. Conversely, confirmed deal signing + mega-cap earnings beats could flip BULLISH if accompanied by >1% rally in NAS100 within 24–48h.
screening.entry_guidance: Market is consolidating into weekend with light conviction. Wait for Iran deal official signing confirmation (likely Monday) and any gap-up follow-through before building long; if NAS100 retakes 29,800–30,000 on deal news, consider modest long on break above. If falls below 29,400 support, reassess to BEARISH on failed recovery signal.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO mega-event
factors.summary: SpaceX IPO closed +19% on Nasdaq Friday, raising $75B and creating euphoria around AI/space capex. Elon Musk became world's first trillionaire, likely attracting retail inflows into NDX. However, this is a single stock event (+2.2T market cap) and does not directly lift NDX unless broader mega-cap earnings cycle improves.

factors.name: Iran deal (geopolitical de-risking)
factors.summary: US-Iran interim peace deal framework nearing closure (per Pakistan PM, finalization 'in next 24 hours'). Strait of Hormuz reopening, $10-20B Iranian asset unlock, and end of regional conflict would reduce energy volatility and lower inflation expectations, supporting equities and lower real yields.

factors.name: AI regulation / export controls tightening
factors.summary: Anthropic suspended Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models per US government directive citing national security. China labeled additional firms 'military companies' by US. These export controls could throttle hyperscaler capex and AI infrastructure demand, pressuring NVDA and semiconductor indices.

factors.name: Inflation / real yields trajectory
factors.summary: No fresh CPI/PCE data in news window. ECB's Nagel noted prices likely to stay elevated even if Iran conflict ends, suggesting sticky inflation. 10Y Treasury and real yields not explicitly mentioned; without yield snapshot, cannot assess multiple compression/expansion risk.

factors.name: Mega-cap earnings calendar
factors.summary: No Q2 earnings releases or guidance updates cited in latest 24h news. CapEx commentary from hyperscalers (AWS, Azure, GCP) absent. Pimco warned defaults rising and equity valuations look stretched, but no major earnings misses reported yet.

factors.name: Credit stress emerging
factors.summary: Pimco flagged rising defaults in debt markets. South Korean media conglomerate JTBC defaulted on securitized loans and was downgraded to junk. Default cycle re-acceleration could weigh on NDX through valuation multiple contraction and credit-sensitive names (FINTECH, discretionary).

factors.name: Sentiment & momentum
factors.summary: NQ +0.46% over 24h but -0.22% in 4h suggests consolidation after SpaceX pop. No clear follow-through; prior three bias reads were all NEUTRAL5/10 with no confirmation, indicating choppy, directionless price action.

factors.name: Crypto correlation (BTC recovery)
factors.summary: Bitcoin rallied to $64,349 session high (from $60,679 Wed low, $59,104 prior week low). Standard Chartered called crypto bottom at ~$59k, expect path to $100k by year-end. Risk-on sentiment in crypto usually precedes equity inflows; however, move still in early recovery phase.

factors.name: Dollar direction
factors.summary: DXY lost 0.27% during week, trading near 99.80. No fresh intraday DXY data in latest window. Weaker dollar typically supports NDX international revenues but signal is soft.

factors.name: VIX / volatility regime
factors.summary: No VIX/VXN quotes provided in news window. Absence of vol data limits conviction on positioning and gamma risk in mega-caps.

screening.narrative: NAS100 consolidates near 29,636 after SpaceX IPO euphoria (+19% Friday) and Iran deal optimism narrowed volatility. Geopolitical de-risking (potential Hormuz reopening, asset unlocks) reduces energy inflation and supports risk-on sentiment, while US AI export controls (Anthropic, China designations) introduce a structural headwind to hyperscaler capex cycle. Credit stress warnings from Pimco and JTBC junk downgrade suggest late-cycle multiple compression risk. Without fresh macro data (CPI, Treasury yields, mega-cap earnings guidance), the market is trapped in a 50-60 pip range, neither confirming bullish breakout nor bearish breakdown.
catalysts_ahead: US economic data releases (CPI, PCE, NFP) — timing not specified but typically mid-week impact
Fed speakers / policy signals (Kevin Warsh now Fed Chair, preferences on rate path unknown)
Iran deal formal signing confirmation (within 24h per Pakistan)
NVIDIA, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN earnings & cloud capex guidance (dates not specified in news)
Semiconductor supply chain updates (TSMC, ASML, Samsung) — no imminent dates in window
Additional US-China trade/tech restrictions or AI regulation announcements
Anthropic IPO timing (currently suspended due to model shutdown, but mentioned as forthcoming)
screening.risk_warning: Bias invalidation if: (1) Iran deal collapses or escalates conflict instead, spiking energy/inflation and forcing Fed hawkish repricing; (2) mega-cap earnings miss guidance and cut capex due to AI regulation / export controls, triggering 3-5% NDX selloff; (3) major CPI surprise hotter than expected, reigniting inflation fears and real yields higher; (4) credit cascade spreads (more junk defaults), forcing risk-off deleveraging in growth; (5) DXY rallies above 100.50, headwind to international revenue earners.
screening.entry_guidance: SpaceX pop and Iran deal framework are moving the market but consolidating after initial enthusiasm. Avoid chasing into resistance at 29,900; wait for pullback to support 29,400 or a fresh macro catalyst (CPI, earnings) before sizing in. Risk/reward currently balanced; move extended by Friday close but not yet extreme.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO mega-event
factors.summary: SpaceX closed +19% on debut, achieving $2T+ market cap and drawing massive retail participation. This represents a significant wealth creation event for tech ecosystem and validates mega-cap tech IPO appetite, but peak euphoria may limit near-term upside.

factors.name: Iran-US peace deal momentum
factors.summary: Pakistan PM claims deal could finalize within 24h, multiple sources report frameworks near completion. Risk-off de-escalation typically supports growth equities and reduces geopolitical premium in yields, lifting valuations.

factors.name: Bitcoin risk-on signal
factors.summary: BTC rallied above $64k on Iran optimism and crypto adoption narratives (Wall Street deepening Ethereum integration, tokenization mirroring ETF boom). NQ-BTC correlation remains strong; risk-on macro favors equities.

factors.name: Fed policy / rate expectations
factors.summary: No major Fed data or guidance in 24h window. Upcoming central bank calendar (BoJ, BoE, RBA, likely FOMC) is a critical unknown; any hawkish surprise would pressure long-duration growth stocks.

factors.name: Real yields / long-duration sensitivity
factors.summary: 10Y Treasury moves not detailed in news window. Iran peace deal could ease inflation expectations, which might support real yields stability or slight compression—modestly bullish for NDX multiples if plays out.

factors.name: AI/chip capex narrative
factors.summary: Anthropic's model suspension due to US export controls is a headwind, but broader AI investment trends (Morpho $175M raise, compute utility focus, 'Mag8' Bitcoin adoption) remain constructive for mega-cap tech and chip demand.

factors.name: Antitrust / regulatory risks
factors.summary: Anthropic disabling Fable 5 and Mythos 5 after US directive signals tightening export controls on AI/chips. Paramount-WBD DOJ approval mixed signal (approval = dovish, but shows scrutiny ongoing). China-US tech tensions rising.

factors.name: Valuation / sentiment extremes
factors.summary: SpaceX priced at $1.77T on $10-15B revenue is a red flag for stretched valuations. Pimco warns 'defaults starting again' and equities valuations 'stretched'; retail SPX/NDX positioning heavy after SpaceX frenzy may presage consolidation.

factors.name: Short-term price action
factors.summary: NAS100 +0.46% in 24h but -0.22% in 4h shows mild consolidation after SpaceX rally. No decisive directional momentum; range-bound behavior suggests market digesting weekend euphoria.

factors.name: Dollar direction
factors.summary: DXY lost 0.27% during prior week as major central bank calendar looms; weak dollar typically supports international revenues for NDX names, but data-dependent on upcoming FOMC/inflation prints.

key_levels.support: 29400–29550 (recent session lows, SpaceX IPO-driven support band)

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 is trapped in a 'post-SpaceX euphoria consolidation' phase after the mega-IPO drove risk-on sentiment and a +19% pop on Friday. The Iran peace deal narrative provides a secondary tailwind, reducing geopolitical premium and supporting growth equities. However, stretched valuations (SpaceX at $1.77T), Pimco's warning on stretched equity multiples, and tightening US export controls on AI/chips create headwinds. The lack of fresh macro catalyst or earnings guidance in the 24h window leaves NAS100 directionless, awaiting next week's major central bank decisions and inflation data to confirm or invalidate the risk-on bias.
catalysts_ahead: FOMC decision (likely within 7-10 days) — hawkish surprise would pressure long-duration growth; dovish would support NDX rally
CPI / Core CPI print (likely early-to-mid next week) — hot surprise = sell-off; cool = continued NDX strength
BoJ, BoE, RBA rate decisions (next 3-5 days) — global yield movements ripple to US growth multiples
Iran deal official announcement (claimed within 24h) — confirmed deal = further geopolitical de-risk; failed talks = risk-off reversal
Mega-cap earnings (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA likely in next 2-3 weeks) — forward guidance on capex and cloud growth critical
Anthropic / OpenAI IPO pipeline news — any AI regulation or funding updates influence sector sentiment
screening.risk_warning: A failed or delayed Iran peace deal would trigger immediate risk-off reversal and could send NAS100 back below 29400. Hotter-than-expected inflation data or hawkish Fed surprise next week would collapse long-duration growth multiples and erase SpaceX rally gains. US export controls on AI chips (Anthropic's suspension is a warning) could accelerate if China tensions escalate, pressuring NVDA, ASML, and downstream mega-cap capex guidance. Finally, retail euphoria around SpaceX IPO may peak out, causing mean-reversion selloff in speculative growth names and QQQ.
screening.entry_guidance: Avoid chasing SpaceX rally; wait for retest of 29550–29650 support before adding longs. If Iran deal fails in next 24h, consider shorts toward 29300 on reversal lower. Hold neutral bias until FOMC/CPI clarity arrives; only commit directional capital after inflation data or Fed guidance materially shifts rate expectations.
